key_insight: A physics-informed neural network can learn transient elastodynamic wave propagation in a bimaterial solid by embedding elasticity equations, interface conditions, and boundary conditions directly into the loss.
engineering_relevance: This provides a fast surrogate for Split Hopkinson Pressure Bar and other high-rate solid mechanics problems, enabling wave-response prediction without rerunning full finite-element simulations.
method: Physics-informed loss with initial, boundary, and interface constraints
Validated and supplemented with ANSYS Explicit Dynamics finite-element data
evidence_signal: The trained model matches finite-element results for wave transmission/reflection, axial and radial displacements, face-averaged responses, and dominant stress-strain histories, while also generalizing to unseen times and modified materials.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful training and FEM-guided validation, so generalization to more complex geometries or stronger nonlinear effects may require additional tuning and data.
paper_title: A Physics-Informed Neural Network Framework for Elastodynamic Wave Propagation in Bimaterial Systems
